definition: To describe or indicate precisely; to outline the exact boundaries or features of something.
usage_notes: Used when precision and clear boundaries matter
Common in technical, legal, and academic writing
Can be used both literally (drawing outlines) and figuratively (defining roles)

etymology.origin_summary: From Latin 'delineare' — to sketch out. 'de-' (completely) + 'lineare' (to draw a line), from 'linea' (line).

examples: The contract clearly delineates the responsibilities of each party.
She delineated the project scope to prevent scope creep.

confusions.difference: 'Describe' gives a general account; 'delineate' implies precise, boundary-level detail.
'Define' states meaning; 'delineate' maps out edges and distinctions.
